NKJV.1: “I am the true vine, and My Father is the vinedresser.
NKJV.2: Every branch in Me that does not bear fruit He takes away; and every branch that bears fruit He prunes, that it may bear more fruit.
NKJV.3: You are already clean because of the word which I have spoken to you.
NKJV.4: Abide in Me, and I in you. As the branch cannot bear fruit of itself, unless it abides in the vine, neither can you, unless you abide in Me.
NKJV.5: “I am the vine, you are the branches. He who abides in Me, and I in him, bears much fruit; for without Me you can do nothing.
NKJV.6: If anyone does not abide in Me, he is cast out as a branch and is withered; and they gather them and throw them into the fire, and they are burned.
NKJV.7: If you abide in Me, and My words  abide in you,  you will ask what you desire, and it shall be done for you.
NKJV.8: By this My Father is glorified, that you bear much fruit;  so you will be My disciples.
NKJV.9: “As the Father  loved Me, I also have loved you; abide in My love.
NKJV.10: If you keep My commandments, you will abide in My love, just as I have kept My Father’s commandments and abide in His love.
NKJV.11: “These things I have spoken to you, that My joy may remain in you, and that your joy may be full.
NKJV.12: This is My  commandment, that you love one another as I have loved you.
NKJV.13: Greater love has no one than this, than to lay down one’s life for his friends.
NKJV.14: You are My friends if you do whatever I command you.
NKJV.15: No longer do I call you servants, for a servant does not know what his master is doing; but I have called you friends,  for all things that I heard from My Father I have made known to you.
NKJV.16: You did not choose Me, but I chose you and appointed you that you should go and bear fruit, and that your fruit should remain, that whatever you ask the Father in My name He may give you.
NKJV.17: These things I command you, that you love one another.
NKJV.18: “If the world hates you, you know that it hated Me before it hated you.
NKJV.19: If you were of the world, the world would love its own. Yet  because you are not of the world, but I chose you out of the world, therefore the world hates you.
NKJV.20: Remember the word that I said to you,  ‘A servant is not greater than his master.’ If they persecuted Me, they will also persecute you.  If they kept My word, they will keep yours also.
NKJV.21: But  all these things they will do to you for My name’s sake, because they do not know Him who sent Me.
NKJV.22: If I had not come and spoken to them, they would have no sin,  but now they have no excuse for their sin.
NKJV.23: He who hates Me hates My Father also.
NKJV.24: If I had not done among them  the works which no one else did, they would have no sin; but now they have  seen and also hated both Me and My Father.
NKJV.25: But this happened that the word might be fulfilled which is written in their law, ‘They hated Me without a cause.’
NKJV.26: “But when the Helper comes, whom I shall send to you from the Father, the Spirit of truth who proceeds from the Father,  He will testify of Me.
NKJV.27: And  you also will bear witness, because  you have been with Me from the beginning.

ESV.1: “I am the true vine, and my Father is the vinedresser.
ESV.2: Every branch in me that does not bear fruit he takes away, and every branch that does bear fruit he prunes, that it may bear more fruit.
ESV.3: Already you are clean because of the word that I have spoken to you.
ESV.4: Abide in me, and I in you. As the branch cannot bear fruit by itself, unless it abides in the vine, neither can you, unless you abide in me.
ESV.5: I am the vine; you are the branches. Whoever abides in me and I in him, he it is that bears much fruit, for apart from me you can do nothing.
ESV.6: If anyone does not abide in me he is thrown away like a branch and withers; and the branches are gathered, thrown into the fire, and burned.
ESV.7: If you abide in me, and my words abide in you, ask whatever you wish, and it will be done for you.
ESV.8: By this my Father is glorified, that you bear much fruit and so prove to be my disciples.
ESV.9: As the Father has loved me, so have I loved you. Abide in my love.
ESV.10: If you keep my commandments, you will abide in my love, just as I have kept my Father's commandments and abide in his love.
ESV.11: These things I have spoken to you, that my joy may be in you, and that your joy may be full.
ESV.12: “This is my commandment, that you love one another as I have loved you.
ESV.13: Greater love has no one than this, that someone lay down his life for his friends.
ESV.14: You are my friends if you do what I command you.
ESV.15: No longer do I call you servants,  for the servant does not know what his master is doing; but I have called you friends, for all that I have heard from my Father I have made known to you.
ESV.16: You did not choose me, but I chose you and appointed you that you should go and bear fruit and that your fruit should abide, so that whatever you ask the Father in my name, he may give it to you.
ESV.17: These things I command you, so that you will love one another.
ESV.18: “If the world hates you, know that it has hated me before it hated you.
ESV.19: If you were of the world, the world would love you as its own; but because you are not of the world, but I chose you out of the world, therefore the world hates you.
ESV.20: Remember the word that I said to you: ‘A servant is not greater than his master.’ If they persecuted me, they will also persecute you. If they kept my word, they will also keep yours.
ESV.21: But all these things they will do to you on account of my name, because they do not know him who sent me.
ESV.22: If I had not come and spoken to them, they would not have been guilty of sin,  but now they have no excuse for their sin.
ESV.23: Whoever hates me hates my Father also.
ESV.24: If I had not done among them the works that no one else did, they would not be guilty of sin, but now they have seen and hated both me and my Father.
ESV.25: But the word that is written in their Law must be fulfilled: ‘They hated me without a cause.’
ESV.26: “But when the Helper comes, whom I will send to you from the Father, the Spirit of truth, who proceeds from the Father, he will bear witness about me.
ESV.27: And you also will bear witness, because you have been with me from the beginning.

NASB.1: 'I am the true vine, and My Father is the vinedresser.
NASB.2: 'Every branch in Me that does not bear fruit, He takes away; and every [branch] that bears fruit, He prunes it so that it may bear more fruit.
NASB.3: 'You are already clean because of the word which I have spoken to you.
NASB.4: 'Abide in Me, and I in you. As the branch cannot bear fruit of itself unless it abides in the vine, so neither [can] you unless you abide in Me.
NASB.5: 'I am the vine, you are the branches; he who abides in Me and I in him, he bears much fruit, for apart from Me you can do nothing.
NASB.6: 'If anyone does not abide in Me, he is thrown away as a branch and dries up; and they gather them, and cast them into the fire and they are burned.
NASB.7: 'If you abide in Me, and My words abide in you, ask whatever you wish, and it will be done for you.
NASB.8: 'My Father is glorified by this, that you bear much fruit, and [so] prove to be My disciples.
NASB.9: 'Just as the Father has loved Me, I have also loved you; abide in My love.
NASB.10: 'If you keep My commandments, you will abide in My love; just as I have kept My Father's commandments and abide in His love.
NASB.11: 'These things I have spoken to you so that My joy may be in you, and [that] your joy may be made full.
NASB.12: 'This is My commandment, that you love one another, just as I have loved you.
NASB.13: 'Greater love has no one than this, that one lay down his life for his friends.
NASB.14: 'You are My friends if you do what I command you.
NASB.15: 'No longer do I call you slaves, for the slave does not know what his master is doing; but I have called you friends, for all things that I have heard from My Father I have made known to you.
NASB.16: 'You did not choose Me but I chose you, and appointed you that you would go and bear fruit, and [that] your fruit would remain, so that whatever you ask of the Father in My name He may give to you.
NASB.17: 'This I command you, that you love one another.
NASB.18: 'If the world hates you, you know that it has hated Me before [it hated] you.
NASB.19: 'If you were of the world, the world would love its own; but because you are not of the world, but I chose you out of the world, because of this the world hates you.
NASB.20: 'Remember the word that I said to you, 'A slave is not greater than his master.' If they persecuted Me, they will also persecute you; if they kept My word, they will keep yours also.
NASB.21: 'But all these things they will do to you for My name's sake, because they do not know the One who sent Me.
NASB.22: 'If I had not come and spoken to them, they would not have sin, but now they have no excuse for their sin.
NASB.23: 'He who hates Me hates My Father also.
NASB.24: 'If I had not done among them the works which no one else did, they would not have sin; but now they have both seen and hated Me and My Father as well.
NASB.25: 'But [they have done this] to fulfill the word that is written in their Law, 'THEY HATED ME WITHOUT A CAUSE.'
NASB.26: 'When the Helper comes, whom I will send to you from the Father, [that is] the Spirit of truth who proceeds from the Father, He will testify about Me,
NASB.27: and you [will] testify also, because you have been with Me from the beginning.

NLT.1: “I am the true grapevine, and my Father is the gardener.
NLT.2: He cuts off every branch of mine that doesn’t produce fruit, and he prunes the branches that do bear fruit so they will produce even more.
NLT.3: You have already been pruned and purified by the message I have given you.
NLT.4: Remain in me, and I will remain in you. For a branch cannot produce fruit if it is severed from the vine, and you cannot be fruitful unless you remain in me.
NLT.5: “Yes, I am the vine; you are the branches. Those who remain in me, and I in them, will produce much fruit. For apart from me you can do nothing.
NLT.6: Anyone who does not remain in me is thrown away like a useless branch and withers. Such branches are gathered into a pile to be burned.
NLT.7: But if you remain in me and my words remain in you, you may ask for anything you want, and it will be granted!
NLT.8: When you produce much fruit, you are my true disciples. This brings great glory to my Father.
NLT.9: “I have loved you even as the Father has loved me. Remain in my love.
NLT.10: When you obey my commandments, you remain in my love, just as I obey my Father’s commandments and remain in his love.
NLT.11: I have told you these things so that you will be filled with my joy. Yes, your joy will overflow!
NLT.12: This is my commandment: Love each other in the same way I have loved you.
NLT.13: There is no greater love than to lay down one’s life for one’s friends.
NLT.14: You are my friends if you do what I command.
NLT.15: I no longer call you slaves, because a master doesn’t confide in his slaves. Now you are my friends, since I have told you everything the Father told me.
NLT.16: You didn’t choose me. I chose you. I appointed you to go and produce lasting fruit, so that the Father will give you whatever you ask for, using my name.
NLT.17: This is my command: Love each other.
NLT.18: “If the world hates you, remember that it hated me first.
NLT.19: The world would love you as one of its own if you belonged to it, but you are no longer part of the world. I chose you to come out of the world, so it hates you.
NLT.20: Do you remember what I told you? ‘A slave is not greater than the master.’ Since they persecuted me, naturally they will persecute you. And if they had listened to me, they would listen to you.
NLT.21: They will do all this to you because of me, for they have rejected the one who sent me.
NLT.22: They would not be guilty if I had not come and spoken to them. But now they have no excuse for their sin.
NLT.23: Anyone who hates me also hates my Father.
NLT.24: If I hadn’t done such miraculous signs among them that no one else could do, they would not be guilty. But as it is, they have seen everything I did, yet they still hate me and my Father.
NLT.25: This fulfills what is written in their Scriptures : ‘They hated me without cause.’
NLT.26: “But I will send you the Advocate — the Spirit of truth. He will come to you from the Father and will testify all about me.
NLT.27: And you must also testify about me because you have been with me from the beginning of my ministry.

WEB.1: “I am the true vine, and my Father is the farmer.
WEB.2: Every branch in me that doesn’t bear fruit, he takes away. Every branch that bears fruit, he prunes, that it may bear more fruit.
WEB.3: You are already pruned clean because of the word which I have spoken to you.
WEB.4: Remain in me, and I in you. As the branch can’t bear fruit by itself, unless it remains in the vine, so neither can you, unless you remain in me.
WEB.5: I am the vine. You are the branches. He who remains in me, and I in him, the same bears much fruit, for apart from me you can do nothing.
WEB.6: If a man doesn’t remain in me, he is thrown out as a branch, and is withered; and they gather them, throw them into the fire, and they are burned.
WEB.7: If you remain in me, and my words remain in you, you will ask whatever you desire, and it will be done for you.
WEB.8: “In this is my Father glorified, that you bear much fruit; and so you will be my disciples.
WEB.9: Even as the Father has loved me, I also have loved you. Remain in my love.
WEB.10: If you keep my commandments, you will remain in my love; even as I have kept my Father’s commandments, and remain in his love.
WEB.11: I have spoken these things to you, that my joy may remain in you, and that your joy may be made full.
WEB.12: “This is my commandment, that you love one another, even as I have loved you.
WEB.13: Greater love has no one than this, that someone lay down his life for his friends.
WEB.14: You are my friends, if you do whatever I command you.
WEB.15: No longer do I call you servants, for the servant doesn’t know what his lord does. But I have called you friends, for everything that I heard from my Father, I have made known to you.
WEB.16: You didn’t choose me, but I chose you, and appointed you, that you should go and bear fruit, and that your fruit should remain; that whatever you will ask of the Father in my name, he may give it to you.
WEB.17: “I command these things to you, that you may love one another.
WEB.18: If the world hates you, you know that it has hated me before it hated you.
WEB.19: If you were of the world, the world would love its own. But because you are not of the world, since I chose you out of the world, therefore the world hates you.
WEB.20: Remember the word that I said to you: ‘A servant is not greater than his lord.’  If they persecuted me, they will also persecute you. If they kept my word, they will also keep yours.
WEB.21: But all these things will they do to you for my name’s sake, because they don’t know him who sent me.
WEB.22: If I had not come and spoken to them, they would not have had sin; but now they have no excuse for their sin.
WEB.23: He who hates me, hates my Father also.
WEB.24: If I hadn’t done among them the works which no one else did, they wouldn’t have had sin. But now have they seen and also hated both me and my Father.
WEB.25: But this happened so that the word may be fulfilled which was written in their law, ‘They hated me without a cause.’
WEB.26: “When the Counselor  has come, whom I will send to you from the Father, the Spirit of truth, who proceeds from the Father, he will testify about me.
WEB.27: You will also testify, because you have been with me from the beginning.

CSB17.1: “I am <sup>ⓒ</sup> the true vine, and my Father is the gardener.
CSB17.2: Every branch in me that does not produce fruit he removes, <sup>ⓓ</sup>and he prunes every branch that produces fruit so that it will produce more fruit.
CSB17.3: You are already clean <sup>ⓔ</sup>because of the word I have spoken to you.
CSB17.4: Remain in me, and I in you. Just as a branch is unable to produce fruit by itself unless it remains on the vine, neither can you unless you remain in me. <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.5: I am the vine;  <sup>ⓖ</sup>you are the branches. The one who remains in me and I in him produces much fruit, <sup>ⓗ</sup>because you can do nothing without me.
CSB17.6: If anyone does not remain in me, he is thrown aside <sup>ⓘ</sup>like a branch and he withers. They gather them, throw them into the fire, <sup>ⓙ</sup>and they are burned. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.7: If you remain in me and my words remain in you, ask whatever you want and it will be done for you. <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.8: My Father is glorified <sup>ⓜ</sup>by this: that you produce much fruit and prove to be <sup>[1]</sup>my disciples.
CSB17.9: “As the Father has loved me, I have also loved you. Remain in my love. <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.10: If you keep my commands you will remain in my love, just as I have kept my Father’s commands and remain in his love. <sup>ⓐ</sup>
CSB17.11: “I have told you these things so that my joy may be in you and your joy may be complete. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.12: “This is my command: Love one another as I have loved <sup>ⓒ</sup> you.
CSB17.13: No one has greater love than this: to lay down his life <sup>ⓓ</sup>for his friends.
CSB17.14: You are my friends if you do what I command you.
CSB17.15: I do not call you servants anymore, because a servant doesn’t know what his master <sup>[2]</sup> <sup>ⓔ</sup>is doing. I have called you friends, because I have made known to you everything I have heard from my Father.
CSB17.16: You did not choose me, but I chose you. <sup>ⓕ</sup>I appointed you to go and produce fruit and that your fruit should remain, so that whatever you ask <sup>ⓖ</sup>the Father in my name, <sup>ⓗ</sup>he will give you. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.17: “This is what I command you: Love one another.
CSB17.18: “If the world hates <sup>ⓙ</sup> you, understand that it hated me before it hated you.
CSB17.19: If you were of the world, the world would love you as its own. However, because you are not of the world, but I have chosen <sup>ⓚ</sup>you out of it, the world hates you.
CSB17.20: Remember the word I spoke to you: ‘A servant is not greater than his master.’ <sup>ⓛ</sup>If they persecuted me, they will also persecute you. If they kept my word, <sup>ⓜ</sup>they will also keep yours.
CSB17.21: But they will do all these things to you on account of my name, <sup>ⓝ</sup>because they don’t know <sup>ⓐ</sup>the one who sent me.
CSB17.22: If I had not come and spoken to them, they would not be guilty of sin. <sup>ⓑ</sup>Now they have no excuse for their sin.
CSB17.23: The one who hates me also hates my Father. <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.24: If I had not done the works <sup>ⓓ</sup>among them that no one else has done, <sup>ⓔ</sup>they would not have sin. <sup>ⓕ</sup>Now they have seen and hated both me and my Father.
CSB17.25: But this happened so that the statement written in their law might be fulfilled: ⓖThey hated me for no reason. [3] ⓗ
CSB17.26: “When the Counselor <sup>ⓘ</sup> comes, the one I will send to you from the Father <sup>ⓙ</sup> — the Spirit of truth <sup>ⓚ</sup> who proceeds from the Father — he will testify about me.
CSB17.27: You also will testify, <sup>ⓛ</sup>because you have been with me from the beginning.

AMP.1: " I am the true Vine, and My Father is the vinedresser.
AMP.2: Every branch in Me that does not bear fruit, He takes away; and every branch that continues to bear fruit, He [repeatedly] prunes, so that it will bear more fruit [even richer and finer fruit].
AMP.3: You are already clean because of the word which I have given you [the teachings which I have discussed with you].
AMP.4: Remain in Me, and I [will remain] in you. Just as no branch can bear fruit by itself without remaining in the vine, neither can you [bear fruit, producing evidence of your faith] unless you remain in Me.
AMP.5: I am the Vine; you are the branches. The one who remains in Me and I in him bears much fruit, for [otherwise] apart from Me [that is, cut off from vital union with Me] you can do nothing.
AMP.6: If anyone does not remain in Me, he is thrown out like a [broken off] branch, and withers and dies; and they gather such branches and throw them into the fire, and they are burned.
AMP.7: If you remain in Me and My words remain in you [that is, if we are vitally united and My message lives in your heart], ask whatever you wish and it will be done for you.
AMP.8: My Father is glorified and honored by this, when you bear much fruit, and prove yourselves to be My [true] disciples.
AMP.9: I have loved you just as the Father has loved Me; remain in My love [and do not doubt My love for you].
AMP.10: If you keep My commandments and obey My teaching, you will remain in My love, just as I have kept My Father's commandments and remain in His love.
AMP.11: I have told you these things so that My joy and delight may be in you, and that your joy may be made full and complete and overflowing.
AMP.12: "This is My commandment, that you love and unselfishly seek the best for one another, just as I have loved you.
AMP.13: No one has greater love [nor stronger commitment] than to lay down his own life for his friends.
AMP.14: You are my friends if you keep on doing what I command you.
AMP.15: I do not call you servants any longer, for the servant does not know what his master is doing; but I have called you [My] friends, because I have revealed to you everything that I have heard from My Father.
AMP.16: You have not chosen Me, but I have chosen you and I have appointed and placed and purposefully planted you, so that you would go and bear fruit and keep on bearing, and that your fruit will remain and be lasting, so that whatever you ask of the Father in My name [as My representative] He may give to you.
AMP.17: This [is what] I command you: that you love and unselfishly seek the best for one another.
AMP.18: "If the world hates you [and it does], know that it has hated Me before it hated you.
AMP.19: If you belonged to the world, the world would love [you as] its own and would treat you with affection. But you are not of the world [you no longer belong to it], but I have chosen you out of the world. And because of this the world hates you.
AMP.20: Remember [and continue to remember] that I told you, 'A servant is not greater than his master.' If they persecuted Me, they will also persecute you. If they kept My word, they will keep yours also.
AMP.21: But they will do all these [hurtful] things to you for My name's sake [because you bear My name and are identified with Me], for they do not know the One who sent Me.
AMP.22: If I had not come and spoken to them, they would not have [the guilt of their] sin; but now they have no excuse for their sin.
AMP.23: The one who hates Me also hates My Father.
AMP.24: If I had not done among them the works (attesting miracles) which no one else [ever] did, they would not have [the guilt of their] sin; but now [the fact is that] they have both seen [these works] and have hated Me [and continue to hate Me] and My Father as well.
AMP.25: But [this is so] that the word which has been written in their Law would be fulfilled, 'They hated Me without a cause.'
AMP.26: "But when the Helper (Comforter, Advocate, Intercessor—Counselor, Strengthener, Standby) comes, whom I will send to you from the Father, that is the Spirit of Truth who comes from the Father, He will testify and bear witness about Me.
AMP.27: But you will testify also and be My witnesses, because you have been with Me from the beginning.

MSG.1: "I am the Real Vine and my Father is the Farmer.
MSG.2: He cuts off every branch of me that doesn't bear grapes. And every branch that is grape-bearing he prunes back so it will bear even more.
MSG.3: You are already pruned back by the message I have spoken.
MSG.4: "Live in me. Make your home in me just as I do in you. In the same way that a branch can't bear grapes by itself but only by being joined to the vine, you can't bear fruit unless you are joined with me.
MSG.5: "I am the Vine, you are the branches. When you're joined with me and I with you, the relation intimate and organic, the harvest is sure to be abundant. Separated, you can't produce a thing.
MSG.6: Anyone who separates from me is deadwood, gathered up and thrown on the bonfire.
MSG.7: But if you make yourselves at home with me and my words are at home in you, you can be sure that whatever you ask will be listened to and acted upon.
MSG.8: This is how my Father shows who he is—when you produce grapes, when you mature as my disciples.
MSG.9: "I've loved you the way my Father has loved me. Make yourselves at home in my love.
MSG.10: If you keep my commands, you'll remain intimately at home in my love. That's what I've done—kept my Father's commands and made myself at home in his love.
MSG.11: "I've told you these things for a purpose: that my joy might be your joy, and your joy wholly mature.
MSG.12: This is my command: Love one another the way I loved you.
MSG.13: This is the very best way to love. Put your life on the line for your friends.
MSG.14: You are my friends when you do the things I command you.
MSG.15: I'm no longer calling you servants because servants don't understand what their master is thinking and planning. No, I've named you friends because I've let you in on everything I've heard from the Father.
MSG.16: "You didn't choose me, remember; I chose you, and put you in the world to bear fruit, fruit that won't spoil. As fruit bearers, whatever you ask the Father in relation to me, he gives you.
MSG.17: "But remember the root command: Love one another.
MSG.18: "If you find the godless world is hating you, remember it got its start hating me.
MSG.19: If you lived on the world's terms, the world would love you as one of its own. But since I picked you to live on God's terms and no longer on the world's terms, the world is going to hate you.
MSG.20: "When that happens, remember this: Servants don't get better treatment than their masters. If they beat on me, they will certainly beat on you. If they did what I told them, they will do what you tell them.
MSG.21: "They are going to do all these things to you because of the way they treated me, because they don't know the One who sent me.
MSG.22: If I hadn't come and told them all this in plain language, it wouldn't be so bad. As it is, they have no excuse.
MSG.23: Hate me, hate my Father—it's all the same.
MSG.24: If I hadn't done what I have done among them, works no one has ever done, they wouldn't be to blame. But they saw the God-signs and hated anyway, both me and my Father.
MSG.25: Interesting—they have verified the truth of their own Scriptures where it is written, 'They hated me for no good reason.'
MSG.26: "When the Friend I plan to send you from the Father comes—the Spirit of Truth issuing from the Father—he will confirm everything about me.
MSG.27: You, too, from your side must give your confirming evidence, since you are in this with me from the start.

NIV.1:
The Vine and the Branches
“I am the true vine, and my Father is the gardener.
NIV.2: He cuts off every branch in me that bears no fruit, while every branch that does bear fruit he prunes so that it will be even more fruitful.
NIV.3: You are already clean because of the word I have spoken to you.
NIV.4: Remain in me, and I will remain in you. No branch can bear fruit by itself; it must remain in the vine. Neither can you bear fruit unless you remain in me.
NIV.5: “I am the vine; you are the branches. If a man remains in me and I in him, he will bear much fruit; apart from me you can do nothing.
NIV.6: If anyone does not remain in me, he is like a branch that is thrown away and withers; such branches are picked up, thrown into the fire and burned.
NIV.7: If you remain in me and my words remain in you, ask whatever you wish, and it will be given you.
NIV.8: This is to my Father’s glory, that you bear much fruit, showing yourselves to be my disciples.
NIV.9: “As the Father has loved me, so have I loved you. Now remain in my love.
NIV.10: If you obey my commands, you will remain in my love, just as I have obeyed my Father’s commands and remain in his love.
NIV.11: I have told you this so that my joy may be in you and that your joy may be complete.
NIV.12: My command is this: Love each other as I have loved you.
NIV.13: Greater love has no one than this, that he lay down his life for his friends.
NIV.14: You are my friends if you do what I command.
NIV.15: I no longer call you servants, because a servant does not know his master’s business. Instead, I have called you friends, for everything that I learned from my Father I have made known to you.
NIV.16: You did not choose me, but I chose you and appointed you to go and bear fruit—fruit that will last. Then the Father will give you whatever you ask in my name.
NIV.17: This is my command: Love each other.
NIV.18:
The World Hates the Disciples
“If the world hates you, keep in mind that it hated me first.
NIV.19: If you belonged to the world, it would love you as its own. As it is, you do not belong to the world, but I have chosen you out of the world. That is why the world hates you.
NIV.20: Remember the words I spoke to you: ‘No servant is greater than his master.’ If they persecuted me, they will persecute you also. If they obeyed my teaching, they will obey yours also.
NIV.21: They will treat you this way because of my name, for they do not know the One who sent me.
NIV.22: If I had not come and spoken to them, they would not be guilty of sin. Now, however, they have no excuse for their sin.
NIV.23: He who hates me hates my Father as well.
NIV.24: If I had not done among them what no one else did, they would not be guilty of sin. But now they have seen these miracles, and yet they have hated both me and my Father.
NIV.25: But this is to fulfill what is written in their Law: ‘They hated me without reason.’
NIV.26: “When the Counselor comes, whom I will send to you from the Father, the Spirit of truth who goes out from the Father, he will testify about me.
NIV.27: And you also must testify, for you have been with me from the beginning.

NRSVCE.1: “I am the true vine, and my Father is the vinegrower.
NRSVCE.2: He removes every branch in me that bears no fruit. Every branch that bears fruit he prunes to make it bear more fruit.
NRSVCE.3: You have already been cleansed by the word that I have spoken to you.
NRSVCE.4: Abide in me as I abide in you. Just as the branch cannot bear fruit by itself unless it abides in the vine, neither can you unless you abide in me.
NRSVCE.5: I am the vine, you are the branches. Those who abide in me and I in them bear much fruit, because apart from me you can do nothing.
NRSVCE.6: Whoever does not abide in me is thrown away like a branch and withers; such branches are gathered, thrown into the fire, and burned.
NRSVCE.7: If you abide in me, and my words abide in you, ask for whatever you wish, and it will be done for you.
NRSVCE.8: My Father is glorified by this, that you bear much fruit and become my disciples.
NRSVCE.9: As the Father has loved me, so I have loved you; abide in my love.
NRSVCE.10: If you keep my commandments, you will abide in my love, just as I have kept my Father’s commandments and abide in his love.
NRSVCE.11: I have said these things to you so that my joy may be in you, and that your joy may be complete.
NRSVCE.12: “This is my commandment, that you love one another as I have loved you.
NRSVCE.13: No one has greater love than this, to lay down one’s life for one’s friends.
NRSVCE.14: You are my friends if you do what I command you.
NRSVCE.15: I do not call you servants any longer, because the servant does not know what the master is doing; but I have called you friends, because I have made known to you everything that I have heard from my Father.
NRSVCE.16: You did not choose me but I chose you. And I appointed you to go and bear fruit, fruit that will last, so that the Father will give you whatever you ask him in my name.
NRSVCE.17: I am giving you these commands so that you may love one another.
NRSVCE.18: “If the world hates you, be aware that it hated me before it hated you.
NRSVCE.19: If you belonged to the world, the world would love you as its own. Because you do not belong to the world, but I have chosen you out of the world — therefore the world hates you.
NRSVCE.20: Remember the word that I said to you, ‘Servants are not greater than their master.’ If they persecuted me, they will persecute you; if they kept my word, they will keep yours also.
NRSVCE.21: But they will do all these things to you on account of my name, because they do not know him who sent me.
NRSVCE.22: If I had not come and spoken to them, they would not have sin; but now they have no excuse for their sin.
NRSVCE.23: Whoever hates me hates my Father also.
NRSVCE.24: If I had not done among them the works that no one else did, they would not have sin. But now they have seen and hated both me and my Father.
NRSVCE.25: It was to fulfill the word that is written in their law, ‘They hated me without a cause.’
NRSVCE.26: “When the Advocate comes, whom I will send to you from the Father, the Spirit of truth who comes from the Father, he will testify on my behalf.
NRSVCE.27: You also are to testify because you have been with me from the beginning.

NET.1: “I am the true vine and my Father is the gardener.
NET.2: He takes away every branch that does not bear fruit in me. He prunes every branch that bears fruit so that it will bear more fruit.
NET.3: You are clean already because of the word that I have spoken to you.
NET.4: Remain in me, and I will remain in you. Just as the branch cannot bear fruit by itself, unless it remains in the vine, so neither can you unless you remain in me.
NET.5: “I am the vine; you are the branches. The one who remains in me — and I in him — bears much fruit, because apart from me you can accomplish nothing.
NET.6: If anyone does not remain in me, he is thrown out like a branch, and dries up; and such branches are gathered up and thrown into the fire, and are burned up.
NET.7: If you remain in me and my words remain in you, ask whatever you want, and it will be done for you.
NET.8: My Father is honored by this, that you bear much fruit and show that you are my disciples.
NET.9: “Just as the Father has loved me, I have also loved you; remain in my love.
NET.10: If you obey my commandments, you will remain in my love, just as I have obeyed my Father’s commandments and remain in his love.
NET.11: I have told you these things so that my joy may be in you, and your joy may be complete.
NET.12: My commandment is this — to love one another just as I have loved you.
NET.13: No one has greater love than this — that one lays down his life for his friends.
NET.14: You are my friends if you do what I command you.
NET.15: I no longer call you slaves, because the slave does not understand what his master is doing. But I have called you friends, because I have revealed to you everything I heard from my Father.
NET.16: You did not choose me, but I chose you and appointed you to go and bear fruit, fruit that remains, so that whatever you ask the Father in my name he will give you.
NET.17: This I command you — to love one another.
NET.18: “If the world hates you, be aware that it hated me first.
NET.19: If you belonged to the world, the world would love you as its own. However, because you do not belong to the world, but I chose you out of the world, for this reason the world hates you.
NET.20: Remember what I told you, ‘A slave is not greater than his master.’ If they persecuted me, they will also persecute you. If they obeyed my word, they will obey yours too.
NET.21: But they will do all these things to you on account of my name, because they do not know the one who sent me.
NET.22: If I had not come and spoken to them, they would not be guilty of sin. But they no longer have any excuse for their sin.
NET.23: The one who hates me hates my Father too.
NET.24: If I had not performed among them the miraculous deeds that no one else did, they would not be guilty of sin. But now they have seen the deeds and have hated both me and my Father.
NET.25: Now this happened to fulfill the word that is written in their law, ‘They hated me without reason.’
NET.26: When the Advocate comes, whom I will send you from the Father — the Spirit of truth who goes out from the Father — he will testify about me,
NET.27: and you also will testify, because you have been with me from the beginning.
